title: Neighbours & Reported Speech
cefrDescriptors: Can report what someone says using indirect speech in the present.
Can talk about neighbours and everyday relations.
concept:
This unit gives you reported speech (le discours indirect) — how to say what someone ELSE said, without quoting them directly. It's everywhere in real talk: "What did she say? — She said she was coming."

The key is the linking word, which changes with the kind of sentence:

1) A STATEMENT → dire que (to say that):
   Direct:   Il dit : « Je suis fatigué. »
   Reported: Il dit qu'il est fatigué.
   (que → qu' before a vowel.)

2) A YES/NO QUESTION → demander si (to ask if / whether):
   Direct:   Elle demande : « Tu viens ? »
   Reported: Elle demande si tu viens.

3) An INFORMATION QUESTION with qu'est-ce que → demander ce que (to ask what):
   Direct:   Il demande : « Qu'est-ce que tu fais ? »
   Reported: Il demande ce que tu fais.
   (Other question words simply stay: demander où, quand, comment, pourquoi…)

Notice the pronouns shift to match the new speaker (je → il/elle), exactly like English ("I'm tired" → he said he was tired).

The theme is neighbours (les voisins) — perfect for reporting little exchanges. Useful verbs: dire, demander, répondre (to answer), expliquer (to explain), raconter (to tell). And to describe relations: saluer (to greet), poli (polite), aimable (friendly), bruyant (noisy).

So you can narrate: « Ma voisine est aimable. Ce matin, elle m'a dit bonjour et elle a demandé si tout allait bien. »

Report two things a friend said today: one statement (… dit que …) and one question (… demande si …).

vocabulary.exampleFr: On se voit sur le palier.
vocabulary.exampleEn: We see each other on the landing.

examples.fr: Le voisin demande ce que je fais.
examples.en: The neighbour asks what I'm doing.

examples.fr: Je salue mes voisins tous les matins.
examples.en: I greet my neighbours every morning.
